basics.name: Dr. Isabella Boux
basics.label: Data Scientist | Cognitive Neuroscientist | Linguist
basics.summary: Data science trainee with a PhD in neurolinguistics and 8+ years of experience analyzing complex behavioral and neurophysiological data. Skilled in R, Python, and Matlab, with strong expertise in statistical modeling, data analysis, and real-world human data. Currently applying machine learning methods to real-world datasets, with a particular interest for eHealth and medicine.

work.name: RWTH Aachen University
highlights: Led three research projects involving controlled experiments in the field of human cognitive neuroscience.

Analized, visualized and interpreted behavioral and neurophysiological data in R, Matlab and Jamovi including descriptive statistics, hypothesis testing (t-tests, ANOVAs, correlations), regression (hierarchical linear and logistic regression) and further procedures (principal component analysis).
Secured Ethics Board approval for conducting experimental studies on human subjects.
Managed the projects' timeline and team, including training of junior memebers
Taught 8 courses in on Neurolinguistics and Experimental Methods in Psychology to over 120 students.
Supervised 10 studends in the conduction of their empirical bachelor thesis projects.

work.name: Free University Berlin
highlights: Implementation from inception to publication of four research projects involving controlled experiments in the field of human cognitive neuroscience.
Ensured data quality across the full lifecycle—from experimental design and data collection to post-collection validation.

Analyzed and visualized behavioral and neurophysiological (electroencephalography) data in Matlab (fieldtrip, EEGlab), Python (MNE-python, seaborn) and R including descriptive statistics, hypothesis testing (t-tests, ANOVAs, correlations) and dimensionality reduction (PCA).
Secured Ethics Board approval for conducting experimental studies on human subjects.
Taught 7 courses on Neurolinguistics and in Linguistics to over 200 students.

work.name: Neurosphere UG
work.summary: Startup company producing a wearable EEG-based neurofeedback tool for relaxation training.
highlights: Analyzed neurophysiological time-series data, including frequency-domain (short fourier trandform) using Python and prototyped interactive data visualizations to improve user experience.
Produced analyses of quantitative survey data to measure product satisfaction and evaluation of user experience with Google Sheets.
Contributed website content translating technical scientific information into accessible language for a broad audience.

work.name: Max Planck Institute for Human Development
highlights: Preprocessed neurophysiological and neuroanatomical data with Matlab (Fieldtrip) and ImageJ.

Programmed computerized experiments with Matlab (Psychtoolbox), ensuring the synchronized operation of data collection devices (e.g., MRI scanner, eye-tracking device, button boxes and experimental task computers) for reliable multi-modal collection of human behavioral and neural data.
Collected data from over  55 human participants, including recruitement, MRI safety screening and data collection in MRI/fMRI, EEG, pupillometry and behavioral experiment.

education.area: Linguistics (with a focus on Cognitive Neuroscience of Language)
education.thesis: Communication beyond form: how the brain processes communicative intention

education.institution: Berlin School of Mind and Brain at Humboldt University of Berlin

awards.title: Doctoral scholarship
awards.summary: Received ~35K EUR for conducting research in the field of Neurosciences in Berlin.

awards.title: Teaching award (co-recipient)
awards.awarder: Philosophical Faculty of the RWTH AAchen University
awards.summary: The proze was awarded for the course 'Experimentalpsychologisches Praktikum', recognizing innovative, research-oriented teaching.

skills.name: Statistics & Inference
keywords: Hierarchical linear modeling / Linear mixed modeling
